summary: basis_curvature_pump_short is a structurally novel pricechange/short fade archetype with all three novel_dim conditions parseable; expected sample is tight but viable, and the asymmetric-tail lessons from R2 favor fixed/breakeven exits over multi_tp/trail/time_only.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Top pricechange/long winners key off taker_buy and global_short_ratio; this is short-side and conditions on a joint basis_rate × premium_bps dislocation plus a magnitude floor (move_pct>=3). No existing archetype in the preview combines basis_rate with premium_bps as a curvature gauge, and short-side pricechange archetypes are under-represented (only ~94 short total across all channels).
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 5000 pricechange events × ~0.40 pump-side (move_pct>=3 captures bulk of pricechange triggers which are by construction large moves) × ~0.20 basis_rate>=0.001 (moderate-to-extreme positive basis) × ~0.30 premium_bps>=20 conditional on positive basis (correlated, so not independent multiplicative) ≈ 120/30d. Conservative given correlation between basis_rate and premium_bps.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Dislocation-fade shorts on pump bursts revert quickly when basis snaps back; favor fixed (the only exit family with positive mean +0.175 across 7320 results) or breakeven for asymmetric tail control. Avoid multi_tp/trail/time_only — all three showed negative means in the coverage summary, and R2's E126 paper-trade trap (TP=0.51/SL=6.00 compounded to -13.5%) warns against asymmetric TP/SL on this kind of mean-reversion thesis. Recommend tight symmetric TP/SL grid with hard_stop.
